final_rules.instructions: ID: 1001. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: Detects double URL encoding, a strong indicator of an evasion attempt. This rule inspects the raw request URI before any decoding and blocks if it contains '%25'. This single rule has a high true positive rate for the provided bypass samples. Monitor for false positives in applications that legitimately transmit encoded data containing '%25'.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Replaced original rule 9 with a simpler, more effective anomaly detection rule. Instead of looking for specific double-encoded characters, this broadly targets the double-encoding indicator ('%25') itself, providing coverage for all sample payloads that use this technique.

final_rules.instructions: ID: 1002. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: Core SQLi detection logic. This rule requires a transformation function to URL-decode and lowercase argument values before inspection. It blocks requests where a common SQL metacharacter (', --, /*, #, ;) and a SQL keyword (select, union, etc.) appear in the same argument value. The `(?i)` flag indicates case-insensitivity.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidated and improved original rules 1, 4, 5, and 8. The new rule uses a more robust regex pattern and applies to normalized (URL-decoded) arguments, making it more accurate and less susceptible to simple whitespace or obfuscation bypasses. This replaces multiple, overlapping rules with a single, more efficient one.

final_rules.instructions: ID: 1003. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: Detects boolean-based SQLi and tautologies like '1 OR 1=1'. Requires URL-decoded arguments. This rule looks for a common tautology expression and a logical operator in the same parameter, accounting for various whitespace encodings.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Refined original rule 2. Instead of matching a static, encoded string like '%2520or%25201%253d1', this rule works on decoded input and uses a more flexible regex to detect the components of a tautology, increasing its resilience to encoding and whitespace variations.

final_rules.instructions: ID: 1004. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: Targets high-confidence functions used in time-based, blind, and error-based SQLi. Requires URL-decoded arguments. The presence of these function calls is a very strong signal of an attack.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Refined and expanded original rule 3. Focused on high-confidence function calls and formatted as a regex for better accuracy. This provides a low-false-positive layer for specific blind SQLi techniques.

final_rules.instructions: ID: 1005. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: Detects SQLi attempts obfuscated with HTML entities. This rule requires arguments to be URL-decoded first to expose the HTML entity string ('&#...'). It then looks for the entity marker in combination with SQL-related keywords.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Kept the logic of original rule 6 but clarified the required transformation (URL-decode). This is a valuable rule for catching a specific class of bypass.

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: catches double-encoded quote-led UNION/SELECT extraction attempts seen in clusters 4 and 6. Pre-processing: use lower() only, since repeated URL decoding is not available in free-plan wirefilter. Deployment note: apply to URI and body. Review for false positives on applications that legitimately pass SQL keywords in parameters.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2527" and (lower(http.request.uri) contains "union" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "select" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "user%2528%2529" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "version%2528%2529")) or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2527" and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "union" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "select" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "user%2528%2529" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "version%2528%2529"))

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: blocks boolean/time-based SQLi bypasses from clusters 2, 3, and 5, including double-encoded OR 1=1, tab-separated OR 1=1, and concatenation-based boolean expressions. Pre-processing: lower() only. These are high-confidence attack strings with low expected legitimate use.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2520or%25201%253d1") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2520and%2520%2528select") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%252509or%2525091%253d1") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2527%252b%257c%257c%252b1%253d1") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2520or%25201%253d1") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2520and%2520%2528select") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%252509or%2525091%253d1") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2527%252b%257c%257c%252b1%253d1")

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: detects high-signal time-based and error-based SQLi primitives from cluster 2. Pre-processing: lower() only. Very low false-positive risk for public web traffic.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2528sleep%25285%2529%2529") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "updatexml%2528") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "concat%25280x3a") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2528sleep%25285%2529%2529") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "updatexml%2528") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "concat%25280x3a")

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: catches comment-injection obfuscation with encoded /* ... */ wrappers around SQL keywords, covering clusters 1 and 4. Pre-processing: lower() only. This is a generalized obfuscation rule; validate against any application that legitimately transports encoded comment tokens in request values.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%252f%252a" and (lower(http.request.uri) contains "union" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "select" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "or" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "--")) or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%252f%252a" and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "union" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "select" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "or" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "--"))

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: catches the authenticated-bypass/login-focused payload family from cluster 7 using double-encoded quote plus comment termination after admin. Pre-processing: lower() only. High-confidence for attack traffic; if your app legitimately sends this exact encoded username pattern, reduce to CHALLENGE instead.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "admin%2527%252b--") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "admin%2527%2520--") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "admin%2527%252509--") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "admin%2527%252b--") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "admin%2527%2520--") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "admin%2527%252509--")

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: detects HTML-entity slash obfuscation and encoded SQL metadata/version probes from clusters 0 and 1. Pre-processing: lower() only. This is a strong anomaly-plus-signature rule for encoded slash entities combined with SQL semantics.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%26%2347%3b" and (lower(http.request.uri) contains "or" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "partition" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "version%2528")) or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%26%2347%3b" and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "or" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "partition" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "version%2528"))

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Purpose: targets fullwidth/unicode-style double-encoded obfuscation mixed with SQL keywords from cluster 0. Pre-processing: lower() only. This is a useful anomaly rule for unusual Unicode encoding combined with SQL terms.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%25ef%25bd" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "%25ef%25bc") and (lower(http.request.uri) contains "select" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "union" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "schema" or lower(http.request.uri) contains "version") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%25ef%25bd" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%25ef%25bc") and (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "select" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "union" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "schema" or lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "version")

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: MEDIUM. Action: CHALLENGE or BLOCK on sensitive endpoints such as login, search, id, and report parameters. Purpose: broad fallback for double-encoded quote plus SQL comment termination, covering multiple clusters. Pre-processing: lower() only. Because this is broader, use carefully to avoid false positives on applications that legitimately transport encoded apostrophes and dashes.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2527" and lower(http.request.uri) contains "--") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2527" and lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "--")

generated_rules.instructions: Severity: MEDIUM. Action: LOG or CHALLENGE. Purpose: anomaly rule for excessive/double encoding patterns commonly used in these bypasses. Pre-processing: lower() only. Since Cloudflare free-plan expressions cannot repeatedly decode input in-rule, this serves as the closest supported compensating control for multi-layer URL encoding. Pair with stricter BLOCK rules above.
generated_rules.rule: (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2525" and lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2527") or (lower(http.request.uri) contains "%2525" and lower(http.request.uri) contains "%252f%252a") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2525" and lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2527") or (lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%2525" and lower(http.request.body.raw) contains "%252f%252a")
